name: Arnold Schwarzenegger
why: The most culturally significant name in bodybuilding; his Encyclopedia remains a foundational training text.

titles: Former world-record powerlifter turned elite bodybuilder

name: Big Ramy
why: Modern mass-monster champion and the first Egyptian bodybuilder to win the Mr. Olympia.
principles: First Egyptian bodybuilder to win the Mr. Olympia, and the second Middle Eastern champion after Samir Bannout in 1983.

name: Bill Pearl
why: Longevity and legacy icon who authored one of bodybuilding's best-selling training books.
principles: Authored "Getting Stronger: Weight Training for Men and Women", which has sold over 600,000 copies.
Also authored "Keys to the Inner Universe", containing roughly 1,500 documented weight-training exercises.
Adopted a lacto-ovo vegetarian diet at age 39, a well-documented personal detail from his own writing.

sources.title: Getting Stronger 20th Anniversary Edition — Shelter Publications (Bill Pearl's own book)

name: Branch Warren
titles: Longtime IFBB Pro, Mr. Olympia top-5 finisher
why: Old-school-style heavy training documented extensively on camera at his own Metroflex Gym.
principles: "[Bodybuilding is] about getting as much blood into the muscle as possible during training, working the muscle to failure" (his own words).
Trained for years at Metroflex Gym in Texas, favoring heavy, controlled compound lifts.
His own advice: be consistent and train with high intensity — the specific split matters less than sticking to it.

sources.title: M&S Legends: How to Train Like a Bodybuilder w/ Branch Warren (direct interview quotes)

name: Brandon Curry
